compiled_state.title: As a developer, I can validate both enhancements work end-to-end using synthetic test fixtures
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Create synthetic test fixtures and run /dso:fix-bug against them to validate that scope-drift detection and intent-conflict detection work as specified in SC7.
**Why**: The individual agent and integration stories verify components in isolation. This story validates the full pipeline end-to-end: a scope-drifting fix is blocked, an incorrect behavioral assumption is caught, and a clean fix passes through.
**Scope**:
- IN: Create 3 synthetic test fixtures: (a) fix that changes a return value not mentioned in ticket → major-drift block, (b) ticket claiming "should return empty list" but 3+ callers handle None → INTENT_CONFLICT pause, (c) fix that only touches investigation-identified files with no behavioral change → no-drift pass. Before/after comparison confirming interception.
- OUT: Agent definitions (Stories 1, 3), workflow integration (Stories 2, 4)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, running /dso:fix-bug against fixture (a) causes the scope-drift reviewer to block with major-drift classification, and running against the pre-change SKILL.md produces no interception for the same fixture
  ← Satisfies: "Validated by running /dso:fix-bug against synthetic test fixtures: fix changes a return value not in ticket → scope-drift reviewer blocks"
- When this story is complete, running /dso:fix-bug against fixture (b) causes intent-search to emit INTENT_CONFLICT and pause investigation, and running against the pre-change agent produces no INTENT_CONFLICT for the same fixture
  ← Satisfies: "Validated by running /dso:fix-bug against synthetic test fixtures: ticket claims should return empty list but 3+ callers handle None → INTENT_CONFLICT pause"
- When this story is complete, running /dso:fix-bug against fixture (c) causes the scope-drift reviewer to permit without friction (no-drift classification)
  ← Satisfies: "Validated by running /dso:fix-bug against synthetic test fixtures: fix only touches investigation-identified files with no behavioral change → no-drift pass"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Testing] Fixtures must be self-contained and runnable in isolation without infrastructure dependencies
- [Testing] Before/after comparison requires running against both pre-change and post-change SKILL.md versions — design fixtures to be deterministic

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
